text:
St Mary's Hospital

Institute of Neurology

Clinic Date: 01 September 2021

Dr Ahmed
St Mary's Hospital
Institute of Neurology, St Mary's Hospital, London W2 1NY

Dear Dr Ahmed,

Sophie Bennett, DOB: 12-03-1992, Hospital No: H583920 NHS No. 9081726345
Flat 3, Rosewood Assisted Living, 14 Brook Street, London, W1K 5DL

Medication:

Levetiracetam 1,000 mg twice daily. Patient-reported adherence is good, with no missed doses in the past month. No adverse effects beyond mild transient irritability in the evenings.

Background and supports:

The patient resides in assisted living and is supported by a key worker; key-worker notes were reviewed where available. These corroborate stable routines, medication prompts, and no safeguarding concerns.

Epilepsy history and current status:

This patient has focal epilepsy. Seizures have historically begun with a rising epigastric sensation and brief speech arrest, progressing on occasion to impaired awareness with right-hand automatisms; there have been no generalised tonic–clonic seizures reported in the past year. Last seizure on 31-May, described as a brief focal aware event lasting approximately 20–30 seconds without loss of awareness, occurring in the late afternoon after a disrupted sleep routine. No injuries. Since then, no further events have been recorded by the patient or noted by the key worker.

Triggers and lifestyle factors:

Patient reports poor sleep and higher work-related stress in late May around the time of the last event. Caffeine intake has been reduced since June and sleep hygiene improved with a consistent bedtime routine, which may have contributed to subsequent stability.

Investigations and monitoring:

Previous MRI (2019) unremarkable. Routine EEG (2020) showed left temporal intermittent rhythmic activity without definitive epileptiform discharges. No new investigations requested today in view of clinical stability. We have asked the key worker to continue logging any spells of altered awareness or behavioural arrest, and to document potential triggers.

Plan:

- Continue levetiracetam 1,000 mg twice daily unchanged.
- Maintain seizure diary (patient and key worker) with attention to sleep, stressors, and any focal aware symptoms.
- Safety advice reviewed; no driving. Occupational risk currently low within supervised environment.
- Consider repeat EEG only if events recur or evolve.

Follow-up:

We will review in six months, or sooner if there are further seizures or concerning changes.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: Last seizure on 31-May... Since then, no further events have been recorded

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Clinic date is 01 September 2021. The letter states: "Last seizure on 31-May... Since then, no further events have been recorded." From 31 May to 01 September is approximately 3 months of seizure freedom. The rule requires at least 6 months to label "seizure free"; since that threshold is not met, we must report frequency rather than seizure-free status. Only one seizure is reported in this period, but no explicit recurring frequency is given. Without a clear numeric frequency per a standard time unit, the frequency cannot be determined precisely.
seizure_frequency_number: Last seizure on 31-May... Since then, no further events have been recorded
